Section 3.3 covers offsets after dispute conclusion, but the justification asserts the deduction is 'repayable to Meridian per Section 3.3'; repayability language actually comes from 3.1, and the claim that promotion-calendar/settlement-history searches were performed is not supported by any cited evidence.

§4.2 says each phase may be deducted once, but the $42,000 chainwide claim ignores that the $9,500 regional phase (up to 900 stores) is a separate earlier phase; escalation cites the 900-store threshold to justify chainwide while both regional+chainwide would total $51,500 near the cap, yet no reconciliation addresses whether regional was already invoiced separately — the single $42,000 approval is asserted without verifying phase separation logic, though arithmetic itself matches the rate.
Names the promo and contract sections, cites the chainwide rate, deduction date within window, 1,240 stores exceeding 900-store threshold, no cap breach or duplicate — leaves no obvious opening.

The justification cites an 'attached distribution confirmation documenting 1,240 stores effective 2025-12-08' and deduction reference NC-105433/date, but no such distribution confirmation or deduction record is present in the cited evidence — invented figures.

The photo evidence corroborating 23 events and the remittance text 'pop forms coming' are cited in the justification but not present in the source evidence provided, and the promotion requires demos on weekends—consistency of the 23-event figure cannot be reconciled against cited evidence, which actually states claims lacking Exhibit B are not payable as submitted (no partial-payment basis shown).

Promo requires demo events on weekends, but the justification pays 23 events on 'various dates' without confirming they were weekend events — a reasonable retailer could dispute whether the photo-corroborated events even meet the weekend performance requirement, and even the photos lack the required signed Exhibit B, so the $5,750 approval rests on documentation §3.4 deems non-payable.

Justification cites store photos of 23 events, the remittance 'pop forms coming' text, and the J. Palmer field-rep email as evidence, but none of these appear in the cited/source evidence; the 23-event corroboration and $5,750 partial figure rest on uncited claims.
All numbers reconcile: 32×$200=$6,400, rate $200<$250 authorized, cap and SKUs match, and 60% precedent (5,550/9,250=60%) yields $6,400×60%=$3,840, consistent with cited evidence.

Names the governing promo, cites contract §3.4 Exhibit B requirement as the pivotal missing item, notes out-of-window overreach, and justifies the 60% via a directly analogous prior settlement — leaving little opening for dispute.

Justification cites store photos from 6 stores, POS scan data totaling 10,540 units, a buyer email, and specific claim details ($6,400, 32 events, $200/event, Dec 20 period) that appear in no cited evidence, and asserts prior case was settled at 60% though the memo shows $5,550/$9,250 = 60% but the analogy claims of scan lift 'across every week' are uncorroborated.
Arithmetic reconciles (30×$250=$7,500; 60%×$7,500=$4,500; $3,000 denied), promo details, cap, dates, and prior-settlement 60% precedent (5,550/9,250=60%) all match cited evidence.

Justification names the governing promo and contract §3.4 Exhibit B requirement, plainly states the missing-documentation basis, cites corroborating evidence and the 60% precedent, and leaves the balance open for reconsideration—leaving no obvious opening.
Justification cites specific store photos (19 of 30), a Meridian field rep email, POS scan figures, remittance note text, deduction ref, and 90-day window—none of which appear in the cited evidence.

The 60% partial rate is justified by photos covering 22 of 35 store-events, but 22/35 = 63%, not 60%, and no cited evidence supports a 60% convention; the numeric rationale does not reconcile with the stated corroboration.

Names the promo and contract §3.4 Exhibit B requirement, states the documentation gap plainly, quantifies corroboration (22 of 35 events, buyer email), and offers a contractual audit path—leaving no obvious opening.

Justification asserts specific uncited facts: store photos from 22 stores, buyer email acknowledgment, POS scan data for PROMO-2026-Q1-008, deduction reference HC-21044, and a 'Meridian partial-settlement convention' — none of which appear in the cited evidence.
